marketState: neutral, weather-driven upside risk capped by a storage surplus and a stretched near-term price
summary: Weather is the dominant fundamental right now: both 1-7D and 8-16D demand are running +28-32% above the 5-year average and pinned above the top of the historical range, unusual heat for the Cooling season. Seasonality is only mildly supportive (56/50/56/63% odds of higher prices over 1-4 weeks, +2.3% typical 4-week move) and its reliability is explicitly flagged Low (51pp spread), so it should not be leaned on heavily. Price already sits modestly above fair value now (+$0.28 front, 'SLIGHTLY OVER') but the fair-value curve itself is projected to rise into the $3.01-$3.15 range over the next 21 days, meaning today's premium largely closes on its own if the seasonal path holds. Storage is the main bearish counterweight: inventories are +175 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +13 Bcf build surprise last week, and daily/weekly technicals (bearish trend, RSI 44-49, negative MACD) show no confirmed bullish momentum yet. Managed money is unusually net short (18th percentile of 5y), which is a bearish positioning signal on its face but also raises the odds of a sharp short-covering move if the heat translates into a below-normal storage build.

longTerm.entry: $3.00-$3.15 (scale in on any dip toward the fair-value zone)
longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk, scale in over 2 tranches
longTerm.rationale: Extreme +30% demand anomaly in both the 1-7D and 8-16D weather windows should pressure storage builds smaller than normal over the coming weeks, working against the current +175 Bcf surplus; seasonality (Sep is historically the strongest month) and a fair-value curve that rises to ~$3.15 by day 21 both support a grind higher, while a heavily net-short managed-money position (18th percentile) adds squeeze potential on any bullish storage surprise.
longTerm.invalidation: Weather anomaly fades back toward the 5y norm, storage prints continue to surprise bearish (builds above forecast), or price closes below $2.80 confirming the daily/weekly bearish trend is intact.

shortTerm.rationale: Near-term signals conflict: weather demand is extremely bullish, but front price is already 'SLIGHTLY OVER' fair value and daily/weekly technicals (trend bearish, RSI sub-50, negative MACD) show no confirmed upside momentum, while the futures curve shows only a mild, unremarkable front-to-next backwardation typical of peak summer.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a daily close above $3.30 with MACD turning positive (buy the breakout with the weather trade), or a pullback into the $2.95-$3.05 fair-value zone (buy the dip); avoid chasing the current premium against a bearish daily/weekly trend.

keyDrivers: 1-7D and 8-16D weather demand +28-32% vs 5y and above the top of the historical range
Fair-value curve rises from $2.93 now to ~$3.15 by day 21, closing today's premium organically
Storage surplus +175 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish build surprise last week
Managed money net short at just the 18th percentile of 5y range (squeeze risk)
Seasonality mildly bullish (56/50/56/63% higher over 1-4 weeks) but explicitly Low reliability
risks: Weather anomaly could fade quickly, removing the main bullish driver and leaving the storage surplus dominant
Daily/weekly technicals remain bearish with no confirmed trend reversal, raising near-term pullback risk
